Inula osmanica, also known as "Jacea osmanica," is a perennial plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Inula osmanica include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and saponins. The most common herbal preparations made with Inula osmanica include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.

parts: Jacea osmanica, also known as the Turkish catmint, is a plant with several medicinal properties. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used in traditional medicine. Jacea osmanica is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas and infusions that are said to have a calming effect on the nervous system. Additionally, Jacea osmanica is used in some cultures to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems.
preparations: Jacea osmanica, also known as golden marjoram, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Herbal preparations of Jacea osmanica are often used to treat digestive issues and anxiety due to its carminative and sedative properties. The plant's essential oil can be extracted through steam distillation and is commonly used in aromatherapy. Golden marjoram is also used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
